title: ctenitis parvula
intro: Ctenitis parvula is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, also known as the wood fern family. It is native to New Zealand and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ctenitis parvula include its potential to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Ctenitis parvula can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
